description: MLIP models used in pressure_scripts. Each entry has the imports needed and a self-contained Python expression that constructs the ASE calculator, consumed directly by pressure_script-generic.py. Calculators must be able to produce stress (chgnet requires compute_stress=True unlike the md_scripts catalog). 'dtype' records the floating-point precision actually used: either set explicitly in calculator_expr (via default_dtype/precision/dtype kwargs), or (where the expr doesn't set it) the package's own default, verified against each package's source. A 'dtype_note' field is present wherever the expr leaves dtype unset, explaining which default applies and why.

models.name: chgnet
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. CHGNet hardcodes TORCH_DTYPE = torch.float32 (no dtype/precision kwarg exists on CHGNet.load or CHGNetCalculator)

models.name: eq-v2-M-omat
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. OCPCalculator/InferenceSettings expose no dtype override (only a tf32 flag) so the checkpoint's own precision applies, and eqV2 checkpoints are trained/saved in float32

models.name: eSEN-30M-OAM
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. Same as eq-v2-M-omat, OCPCalculator has no dtype override so the eSEN checkpoint's native float32 precision applies

models.name: grace-mp
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. grace_fm forwards **kwargs to TPCalculator, whose float_dtype default is 'float64'

models.name: grace-oam
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. Same as grace-mp, TPCalculator's float_dtype default is 'float64'

models.name: mattersim-v1-5M
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr; MatterSimCalculator's dtype kwarg defaults to 'float32'

models.name: nequip
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr; from_compiled_model has no dtype override, so precision is whatever was baked into the compiled checkpoint at export time (nequip's model_dtype default is 'float32')

models.name: pet-oam-xl
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. UPETCalculator's dtype kwarg defaults to None, which keeps the checkpoint's native precision (PET architecture's __supported_dtypes__ default is float32)

models.name: pet-omat-xl
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. Same as pet-oam-xl, dtype defaults to None (checkpoint's native float32 precision)

models.name: uma-m-omat
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. get_predict_unit/InferenceSettings expose no dtype override (only a tf32 flag), and UMA checkpoints are trained/saved in float32

models.name: uma-s-omat
models.dtype_note: not set in calculator_expr. Same as uma-m-omat, no dtype override available and UMA checkpoints are float32
